items.title: Iran's race: Break the US blockade or risk its economic lifeline

children.content: Analysis of how much oil Iran is producing, shipping and having to store shows pressure is mounting on Tehran, but while experts agree the US blockade is effective, there are questions about whether it can really force Iran back to the negotiating table.

articles.title: Trump 'not satisfied' with Iran's new proposal to end the war

children.content: Peace talks between the United States and Iran have stalled, with US President Donald Trump threatening to "blast them to hell" if they don't reach a deal.

image.altText: Iranian foreign minister Abbas Aragachi arriving with his team in Pakistan after they get off the plane.

children.content: More than 100 activists, including six Australians,
on ships bound for Gaza were intercepted by Israeli forces earlier this week

items.title: Shift in the Middle East has implications for countries like Australia

children.content: Donald Trump's earlier signalling that he will neither walk away from the war declaring a victory or escalate it, but will try to starve the Iranians out, suggests there is no clear sign of how this war could end.

image.altText: An older man from the UAE in a white robe and head covering looks slightly concerned. The background is blue.

items.title: How a 'loner' went from a life of privilege to Bondi gunman

children.content: Sajid Akram was a man of unusual secrecy — even to his own family. New details uncovered by Background Briefing shed light on his downward spiral into violence.

image.altText: BB bondi ep1 main

items.title: Police pulled him over on his farm. He was tasered 30 seconds later

children.content: Disturbing cases of people wrongly tasered by police on their farm and at school has raised questions over whether officers are too reliant on the devices.

items.title: One year into his second term as PM, Albanese says US 'playing a different role'

children.content: Anthony Albanese says the United States is "playing a different role" in the world under President Donald Trump, forcing Australia and allies to adjust how they respond to unexpected conflicts.

image.altText: The prime minister makes a point with his hands extended speaking into an ABC microphone in a radio studio.

items.title: Gas vs beer: How Pocock's 57 seconds caused the government a tax headache

children.content: David Pocock was struggling to find cut-through on the issue of gas taxes, but then a question about beer excise sparked a social media war of words.

items.title: What is a town camp and what is life like there for Aboriginal people?

children.content: Kumanjayi Little Baby is believed to be taken from her bed in the night while visiting a town camp near Alice Springs with her mother. This is how town camps came to be and what it is like to live in one.

articles.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family calls for calm following Alice riots

children.content: The family of Alice Springs five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby have called for calm following rioting in the outback town overnight, while police say her alleged murderer was beaten unconscious.

items.title: 'No way to treat close partners': Trump hikes tariffs on EU cars to 25%

children.content: US President Donald Trump says he's ordered the higher tariff because the European Union was "not complying with our fully agreed to Trade Deal".

image.altText: A man prepares to board a helicopter

items.title: The four women who left 'Australia Street' to try and leave Syria

children.content: Four Australian women, their children and grandchildren are stuck in limbo in Syria after leaving a refugee camp for family members of suspected Islamic State fighters. This is what we know about them.

items.title: Water restrictions loophole has some big players still pumping, expert warns

children.content: A loophole in the NSW government's water restrictions in the northern basin of the Murray Darling means river water is still being sucked up by a handful of major licence holders.

items.title: Tie property tax changes to income tax cuts, productivity chief says

children.content: Productivity Commission chair Danielle Wood says there is a "case" for property tax changes, but for it to be serious reform, the economic benefit must flow to workers.

image.altText: A sold sticker on a real estate listing board.

items.title: Melbourne Storm lose seven in a row for the first time in club history

children.content: The Melbourne Storm have sunk to a historic low, losing seven consecutive NRL matches for the first time as the Dolphins powered to victory at a wet Lang Park in Brisbane.

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

items.title: Hard-won legacy of Australia's longest strike in jeopardy

children.content: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

items.title: Suspect charged after stabbings of Jewish men in London

items.title: Lessons should have been learnt prior to NDIS rollout, integrity boss says

children.content: About $3.7 billion of National Disability Insurance Scheme money was handed out inappropriately last financial year, the scheme's integrity chief has told a parliamentary hearing.

items.title: Bondi royal commission interim report careful but ultimately damning

children.content: The message beneath the restrained language of the report investigating the Bondi terror attack is that by Hanukkah 2025, Australia had grown dangerously complacent about terrorism – and about the safety of the Jewish community.

items.title: Banksy behind overnight reveal of new statue in central London

children.content: Elusive street artist Banksy has confirmed he is behind a new work drawing crowds in central London: a statue of a suited man striding into the void while holding a flag that blocks his view.
